mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity that supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above its 12-month trend yields negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in > 65% of instances following CPIAUCSL increases above 0.4 percent over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals contracting supply-side activity that immediately triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month trend.
